items.headline: Goodman still interested in Derby if invited -- and if hand improves

news_items.headline: Ohtani (knee) scratched from start, to miss ASG
news_items.summary: Shohei Ohtani was scratched from his scheduled pitching start Friday due to continued irritation in his left knee and will skip the All-Star Game with the hope of ensuring he is "in a good place to be able to be in the rotation."

news_items.headline: Red Sox plane issues force delayed start vs. Mets
news_items.summary: The Red Sox pulled out a 6-2 win Friday night despite arriving at Citi Field a little more than two hours before the scheduled first pitch after the team plane was grounded for nearly 24 hours due to mechanical issues.

news_items.headline: Peters records first White Sox cycle since Abreu
news_items.summary: Rookie Chicago White Sox center fielder Tristan Peters hit for the cycle Friday night, becoming the franchise's first player to achieve the feat in nine years.

news_items.headline: Sources: Cards' Wetherholt gets $112.5M deal
news_items.summary: Infielder JJ Wetherholt and the Cardinals are in agreement on an eight-year, $112.5 million contract extension, sources told ESPN.

news_items.headline: Phillies' Schwarber latest Home Run Derby add
news_items.summary: Phillies slugger Kyle Schwarber and White Sox rookie Munetaka Murakami have been named as participants in the Home Run Derby, filling out the eight-man field for the contest in Philadelphia on Monday night.

news_items.headline: Herrera, Murakami among All-Star replacements
news_items.summary: Cardinals catcher Iván Herrera was named Friday to the National League All-Star team as an injury replacement for Los Angeles Dodgers two-way star Shohei Ohtani.

news_items.headline: Jays' Okamoto ties Ohtani's mark with 22nd HR
news_items.summary: Toronto's Kazuma Okamoto hit his 22nd home run Friday night to tie Shohei Ohtani for the most by a Japanese-born rookie in a season in the majors.

news_items.headline: Rockies rally in ninth, survive Giants’ comeback bid for 4-3 win
news_items.summary: Colorado scored three times in the ninth, then turned to Juan Mejia for the final out with the bases loaded.

news_items.summary: This. THIS is what a complete Rays win feels like. Taking advantage of opponent’s mistakes, crisp baserunning and a defensive clinic. news_items.headline: Soggy bottom: Braves wait around, find no joy in 2-1 loss
news_items.summary: Ah yes we haven’t had a “HR/FB and ballpark dimensions are stupid” loss in a while

news_items.headline: Dodgers plagued by pitching struggles, defensive mistakes in loss to Arizona
news_items.summary: Shohei Ohtani and Andy Pages hit back-to-back home runs in the first inning, but little goes right from there in a 9-3 loss to the Arizona Diamondbacks.

news_items.headline: 'Very manly' Okamoto ties Ohtani's rookie record -- and it's not even the All-Star break
Bullpen game marred by sloppy defense
news_items.summary: After Ohtani was scratched from his start due to knee irritation, the bullpen struggled to contain Arizona's offense

news_items.headline: Walker, Crooks Lead Cardinals Over Braves on a Rain-Soaked Friday Night
news_items.summary: Friday night’s game between the St. Louis Cardinals and the Atlanta Braves was really two completely different stories. There’s the first third of the game which featured a pitcher’s duel between Kyle Leahy for the Cardinals and Chris Sale for the Braves. The final two-thirds of the game didn’t begin until a 2-hour rain delay […]

news_items.headline: Okamoto Homers, Jays Win
news_items.summary: Blue Jay 5 Padres 3 A three-game win streak. A few days ago that would have seemed impossible. We owe this one to Kazuma Okamoto, who hit a three-run home run in the fifth.
